chapters.title: How you see the world shapes what the world sends your way
chapters.text:
Life moves in an endless spiral, and if you are not careful, you will find yourself running the same circles over and over. Even the worst situation can teach you something. Many people spend their lives focused on the bad things that might happen to them. That mindset breeds fear.

Fear is a barrier standing between you and your potential. Once you remove fear, you will see that you can take plenty of positives from a negative situation. Some of the world's greatest figures, from Walt Disney and Albert Einstein to Michael Jordan and Oprah Winfrey, all learned from tough times. You cannot let negativity take over. Some of the silly things you do can also be turned into lessons that make you a better person, and that is why this book matters.

There is no single formula for lasting happiness. You have to make the best of your current situation and look forward to better days.

Chip Gaines, an experienced writer, has used his own life as an example of how to learn from doing silly things. If you want to know how to make the best of a bad situation, this is the right book for you.

chapters.title: When you see the positives, it is hard for the negative to get to you
chapters.text:
Looking at things from a positive angle clears out negative feelings.

Chip Gaines was a slow learner in first grade. At the age of seven and a half, he had not yet learned the basics of the alphabet, but he was not particularly bothered by it. His school split the first-graders into four groups: Eagles, Falcons, Blue Jays, and Penguins. Gaines was placed in the Penguin group, and when it was time for reading class, the Penguins were taken to the gym instead.

Even though penguins were the only birds in those groups that could not fly, Gaines never saw it as something to be ashamed of. Instead, he saw it as being special and unique. He believed he was special too, and that is why he refused to dwell on the negative side of his slow start.

Failing to believe in yourself is the start of a lifelong inferiority complex. The best way to stay positive is to believe in yourself.

Growing up, Gaines had always loved baseball like it was his life. He shared the passion with his dad, and together they played and practiced. Gaines's love for baseball grew into an obsession, and it almost caused his downfall.

He paid maximum attention to baseball in school and made the college team. Thinking he was on his way to becoming a professional baseball player, he was cut in his first year in college. The sudden change hit him hard, because he had already tied his entire identity to the prospect of being a baseball player.

When you attach your self-worth to a single thing, you put yourself at risk of falling hard.

Gaines knew he could not keep wallowing in self-pity, so he decided to move on. He worked at a lawnmower company, and it was there he found another passion: being an entrepreneur.

Sometimes your first passion is only a means to an end. Gaines's original plan to become a baseball player made him so disciplined with his work, and that discipline would help him later in life.

For some context, in 2016, in its first full year of operation, Magnolia Market reported an estimated 1.9 million visitors.

chapters.title: Taking calculated risks matters because your choices affect the people around you
chapters.text:
Gaines started three small businesses in Texas, one of which he ran with a few Mexican partners. They could communicate with him in English, but he could not communicate with them in Spanish. He felt he needed to learn the language as a way of showing respect to his business partners, so he took a Spanish class in Mexico.

He left the larger part of his businesses in the hands of his girlfriend, Joanna, and he went on the trip to Mexico. While Gaines was away, his businesses nearly folded because Joanna was not fully prepared to handle them. This experience made Gaines understand that running a business was not as easy as he had thought.

Being an entrepreneur requires being in charge of everything.

An entrepreneur has to know about every part of the business because if anything goes wrong, he bears most of the damage.

Gaines's marriage to Joanna taught him the power of love and commitment. He believes that love is more than a romantic journey. It is a force that should be used to develop positive energy.

On Gaines's forehead, there is a scar in the middle shaped like a wishbone. The scar came from taking a new four-wheeler cart up a steep hill and landing on his face after losing control. In that moment, as he thought about what could have gone wrong, he came to terms with the size of his role in his family's life. With a wife and five children, Gaines realized that while it is good to have fun, taking calculated risks is the smartest way to survive.

Being a father and a husband carries a lot of responsibility, and after a close call with disaster, he realized that every choice he made would affect the people around him.

chapters.title: Fear holds you back by making risk feel like a mistake
chapters.text:
Life has a way of throwing curveballs no matter how much you plan. You might have plans A, B, and C, but life will show up with a plan D. There is no ceiling on what you can achieve as long as you drop fear and understand the logic behind taking risks.

Risks are designed to scare you off big things, and the moment you let fear win, you will never see your true potential.

Many people spend their days living in fear of the unknown and avoiding risks, yet life still finds its way to them, sometimes in painful ways.

Joanna was pregnant with the couple's second child when she told Gaines one day that she thought it was time to close her Magnolia Market. The Magnolia Market was her small shop, and it was starting to grow with more customers. When he asked why she had made the decision, Joanna said she felt it in her gut. She explained that she had to give her kids her full attention. Joanna was willing to give up one dream to fulfill another, and Gaines supported her decision.

Sometimes letting go of a winning situation does not mean you are losing. It is okay to give up one dream to chase another, as long as the new dream is valid.

After the birth of their fourth child, the couple agreed to reopen the Magnolia Market, and Joanna went back to business.

After reopening the Magnolia Market, the couple launched an online market. They did this at the same time as filming the first season of the Home and Garden Television Network (HGTV) show Fixer Upper.

As they grew, the Magnolia Market expanded and needed more space and bigger warehouses.

Their growth had started to make them uncomfortable, but as an experienced person, Gaines knew how to make the best of every situation. Their TV show also gained a lot of viewers, and soon they had people from across the world visiting the Magnolia Market in Waco, Texas.

The growth of the business brought challenges, as they struggled to find a bigger warehouse and keep up with the demand from customers.

Bigger businesses bring bigger challenges, and it takes commitment and determination to overcome them. As they looked for a solution, Joanna found a big property with two huge silos, and they bought it.

Despite the high cost of the property and the renovation it would take, the couple purchased it and spent a fortune on it. They were not scared of running at a loss, because even if they did, they would both see it as an experience and move on.

The property ended up solving a big part of their problem, and they were able to operate the business well.

chapters.title: Choices that feel small today can grow into the next big thing tomorrow
chapters.text:
This was clear in the purchase of the Silo property. Gaines and Joanna had decided to stay in Waco permanently to look after their business. The couple were still filming with HGTV, and they were firm in their decision to not move from Waco. HGTV felt Waco was too remote and underdeveloped, but the couple believed that if HGTV needed them, the network would come to them.

Fortunately, the show picked up massively and attracted visitors to the silo, bringing unprecedented development to Waco.

The single act of purchasing the silo property set off a chain of reactions from people that worked for the greater good of Waco.

Gaines describes himself as a handsome yet scrappy man. In this context, scrappy does not mean unkempt and uncoordinated. It means being tough and determined, never giving up.

When hiring for the Magnolia Market, Gaines and his wife make sure they bring on people who are hard-working and proactive.

If you figure out what drives you, run, do not walk, in the direction of making real, fulfilling work out of that drive. Make sure you have a dream and accept the failures that come your way as you plan to achieve that dream.

The Gaineses taught their employees that money cannot teach passion, and that the best way to be productive is to be passionate about what you do.

Passion brings a sense of belonging that effectively boosts your production in any situation you find yourself in.

Gaines's deal with HGTV took a lot of time and effort. The deal highlighted his dislike for being controlled. HGTV controlled most of the actions, and this made him uncomfortable. The couple were running the Magnolia Market, the Fixer Upper show, and a family all at once. HGTV provided them with a lot of money and privileges, but it was wearing on their personal lives.

You need to examine and re-examine your position at regular intervals to know where you stand. You need to set priorities in life, and regardless of what happens, you need to be courageous enough to make a decision.

chapters.title: Knowing the difference between a job and a piece of work matters
chapters.text:
A job is like a task that has to be done for an agreed price, while work is the effort and commitment poured into reaching a goal.

Knowing which one fits you between a job and work is very helpful. Once you find your dream, chase it. You make it.

The couple decided to quit Fixer Upper and focus on the Magnolia Market and the family.

You have to learn to include people with different views in your circle. Surrounding yourself only with people whose ideas match yours limits your exposure. Gaines believes that without growth, there is no change. You cannot really grow in life if you do not extend your views beyond what is right in front of you.

The willingness to put your thoughts into action separates you from the novice.

The novice will stay a novice as long as his thoughts do not move beyond his head.

Gaines's dream of becoming a living pathway for other people became a reality because he was active about his dreams. Your actions, to a large extent, affect the lives of those around you. When you are inactive, you are ineffective.

You have to carry integrity, character, and faithfulness if you want to be successful in life.

Gaines's legacy teaches the importance of selflessness. You cannot sit idle and expect your dreams to unfold before your eyes.

As Winston S. Churchill once put it, success is not final, failure is not fatal, and it is the courage to continue that counts.

chapters.title: Final thoughts
chapters.text:
Failing with courage is better than being scared of taking risks. If you fail, you will surely learn something. You also have to remember that in chasing your passion, your age does not matter. Failure is an important, inevitable phase of our lives. It is a way of gaining experience and learning about life in ways that would change the way we think.

Our ability to fail is built in, and so is our ability to rise from failure. Whenever we fall, we should not stay down and wallow in pity. The best motivation we can give ourselves is to keep pushing. When we falter and refuse to stand, we will never know what made us fall and how to avoid it the next time.

Our ability to survive also depends on our decision-making skills. Nobody is an expert at making decisions, and sometimes failures teach us how to make the best ones. When we learn how to plan our moves using our past experience as a roadmap, we find it easier to reach success. Trusting your gut means you are willing to take calculated risks. You have to find a way to learn from your silly mistakes and apply these lessons to every part of your life.

Try this. Take time to evaluate your current position and ask yourself if you love your current job, or if you would rather start your own work. You have to be able to make decisions for yourself and those around you, no matter how tough it may seem.

Made it to the end?
